title: Crispy Chinese Shrimp Wonton Soup
description: A festive, briny soup featuring crispy shrimp wontons and a glassy-jelly broth with chili flakes.
instructions: In a bowl, combine minced shrimp, soy sauce, ginger, garlic, salt, pepper, and chili flakes until well coated.
Spoon 1 tablespoon of shrimp mixture onto the center of a wonton wrapper, moisten the edges with water, fold into a triangle and seal edges by pinching together.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat, add a small splash of sesame oil, and fry wontons until edges are golden and crisp, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer fried wontons to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
In a medium saucepan, whisk together chicken broth, water, rice vinegar, and agar-agar powder until dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to maintain a steady simmer for 5 minutes, allowing the agar-agar to activate.
Add the crispy wontons to the simmering broth, letting them warm through for 2 minutes.
Ladle soup into bowls, drizzle with remaining sesame oil, and sprinkle sliced scallions on top.
Adjust seasoning with extra salt or chili flakes if desired.
Serve immediately while the broth remains clear and slightly thickened.
